title: Excitation functions for Sc radioisotopes production via (alpha,x) nuclear reactions on natural calcium up to 25 MeV

x4freetext: Excitation functions for Sc radioisotopes production
via (alpha,x) nuclear reactions on natural calcium up
School of Nuclear Science and Engineering;

of Chemistry and Applied Biomedical Sciences

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Radiation Physics and Chemistry, Vol.227, p.112361 (2025)

x4freetext: NatCaCO3 powder (purity: 99.5%) was used as calcium
target samples. The samples were prepared by
sedimentation technique and followed by compaction
wrapped in thin aluminium foil, which strengthens
mechanically. The thicknesses of the formed CaCO3
layer were 4.48-11.15 mg/cm2 for the targets. Three
stacks of samples with different configurations were
used. The natCu and natTi foils were used as beam
type GC1020) with a multi-channel analyzer was used.
Stacks were irradiated for 1 hour under
The total uncertainty of the beam energy at each foil
was estimated using the SRIM software by the
uncertainties of the primary beam energy (0.3 MeV) and
The obtained results were critically compared
with previous experimental reports and with
theoretical data obtained by the EMPIRE-3.2.3 code
Entry was approved by Dr. L.A. Hasnowo.
